channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18438] sanity test_398p: @@@@@@ FAIL: aio copy 1 w/bsize 4096 failed: 1
item.description:
Lustre Gerrit Janitor
..
Initial testing failed: IMPORTANT: these tests appear to be new failures unique to this patch
..
test_398p(Seen in reviews: 54073 53997 53912 53857 53835 53802 53719 53202 53051 53035 52794 52566 52266 52167 52113 52087 41308)

Regular sanity.sh:test_389p crash may be a result of a client eviction caused by the following race:

dd
  cl_io_loop
    cl_io_lock
      ldlm_cli_enqueue_fini
        ldlm_lock_enqueue
          ldlm_grant_lock.   <<< ldlm lock is granted here, and is held until call to osc_lock_detach below
          
    cl_io_start
      vvp_io_write_start
        vvp_io_write_commit
          osc_io_commit_async
            osc_queue_async_io
              osc_extent_find
                for (; ext; ext = next_extent(ext)) {
                  if (overlapped(ext, cur)) {
                    conflict = osc_extent_get(ext);

                if (conflict != NULL) {
                  rc = osc_extent_wait(env, conflict, OES_INV);  <<< this assumes waiting for completion of RPC in progress
                }

    cl_io_end
    cl_io_unlock
      osc_lock_cancel
        osc_lock_detach.  <<< ldlm lock granted above is released

Writing of conflict extent may get blocked if tmax_rpcs_in_flight lockless write rpcs try to obtain lock on server, blocking ast gets sent to the client, but the lock can not be cancelled. That leads to the client eviction.

 

Here are log (https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54625) excerpts which match the above description:

Server sends blocking ast:

00010000:00010000:2.0:1711703057.781579:0:4369:0:(ldlm_lock.c:701:ldlm_add_bl_work_item()) ### lock incompatible; sending blocking AST. ns: filter-lustre-OST0001_UUID lock: ffff8800789d98c0/0x84b3485a578c344e lrc: 2/0,0 mode: PW/PW res: [0x280000402:0xc32f:0x0].0x0 rrc: 149 type: EXT [7340032->40894463] (req 7340032->7348223) gid 0 flags: 0x40000000000000 nid: 192.168.204.11@tcp remote: 0x4d0bec30efe9ee3c expref: 156 pid: 8441 timeout: 0 lvb_type: 0
00010000:00010000:2.0:1711703057.787862:0:4369:0:(ldlm_lockd.c:494:ldlm_add_waiting_lock()) ### adding to wait list(timeout: 100, AT: on) ns: filter-lustre-OST0001_UUID lock: ffff8800789d98c0/0x84b3485a578c344e lrc: 4/0,0 mode: PW/PW res: [0x280000402:0xc32f:0x0].0x0 rrc: 152 type: EXT [7340032->40894463] (req 7340032->7348223) gid 0 flags: 0x70000400000020 nid: 192.168.204.11@tcp remote: 0x4d0bec30efe9ee3c expref: 158 pid: 8441 timeout: 10054 lvb_type: 0

Watchdog reports that the thread is stuck as it is waiting for lock cancel:

 9995.001938] Pid: 4369, comm: ll_ost_io00_003 3.10.0-7.9-debug #1 SMP Sat Mar 26 23:28:42 EDT 2022
[ 9995.001939] Call Trace:
[ 9995.002054] [<0>] ldlm_completion_ast+0x963/0xd00 [ptlrpc]
[ 9995.002088] [<0>] ldlm_cli_enqueue_local+0x259/0x880 [ptlrpc]
[ 9995.002144] [<0>] tgt_extent_lock+0xea/0x2a0 [ptlrpc]
[ 9995.002187] [<0>] tgt_brw_lock.isra.29+0x143/0x320 [ptlrpc]
[ 9995.002229] [<0>] tgt_brw_write+0x6a5/0x2790 [ptlrpc]
[ 9995.002264] [<0>] tgt_request_handle+0x74e/0x1a50 [ptlrpc]
[ 9995.002292] [<0>] ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x26c/0xcb0 [ptlrpc]

Server evicts the client after 100s:

00010000:00020000:2.0:1711703157.930758:0:15532:0:(ldlm_lockd.c:261:expired_lock_main()) ### lock callback timer expired after 100s: evicting client at 192.168.204.11@tcp  ns: filter-lustre-OST0001_UUID lock: ffff8800789d98c0/0x84b3485a578c344e lrc: 3/0,0 mode: PW/PW res: [0x280000402:0xc32f:0x0].0x0 rrc: 154 type: EXT [7340032->40894463] (req 7340032->7348223) gid 0 flags: 0x60000400000020 nid: 192.168.204.11@tcp remote: 0x4d0bec30efe9ee3c expref: 160 pid: 8441 timeout: 10054 lvb_type: 0

client is evicted:

00000100:02020000:0.0:1711703159.091792:0:18173:0:(import.c:1557:ptlrpc_import_recovery_state_machine()) 167-0: lustre-OST0001-osc-ffff880081c63800: This client was evicted by lustre-OST0001; in progress operations using this service will fail.

client’s dd:osc_extent_find() continues after eviction, gets -5 from osc_extent_wait(env, conflict, OES_INV) and releases the lock:

00000008:00000001:1.0:1711703159.101355:0:13118:0:(osc_cache.c:817:osc_extent_find()) Process leaving via out (rc=18446744073709551611 : -5 : 0xfffffffffffffffb)
00010000:00010000:1.0:1711703159.101595:0:13118:0:(ldlm_lock.c:906:ldlm_lock_decref_internal()) ### final decref done on CBPENDING lock ns: lustre-OST0001-osc-ffff880081c63800 lock: ffff88012c0b3a80/0x4d0bec30efe9ee3c lrc: 3/0,0 mode: PW/PW res: [0x280000402:0xc32f:0x0].0x0 rrc: 146 type: EXT [7340032->40894463] (req 7340032->7348223) gid 0 flags: 0x810420400000000 nid: local remote: 0x84b3485a578c344e expref: -99 pid: 13118 timeout: 0 lvb_type: 1
item.summary: sanity test_398p: @@@@@@ FAIL: aio copy 1 w/bsize 4096 failed: 1

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are closed can be reopened.

comments.comment: duplicate of LU-17190

Yes, we solved this deadlock by using high prior I/O under BAST (LU-17190).
The topmost patch is https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56327/.

This bug can be easily reproduced with hybrid BIO and DIO (using server-side locking) case.
qian_wc I believe you have a bug/patch related to this?

"Vladimir Saveliev <vladimir.saveliev@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56956
Subject: LU-18438 osc: do not osc_extent_wait on conflicting extent
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 5cd2239b73634f6faeb020342f46fcccc04d6fb6
